questions.full_maxc_copy_present: YES -- E_49152_32_3 + ...3n1 = ~1.5ms/token full-MAXC canonical-store materialization, in BOTH the owned route and gqa
questions.ms_per_token_after_fo2: ~1.5ms GPU-busy (FO2 removed the fp32->fp16 CAST copy, NOT the canonical-store materialization)
questions.on_critical_path: PRIOR (gqa-era) MAXC-shrink showed +1.5ms/+8 tok/s; BUT the owned route changed the overlap (gpu_busy 13.7 vs wall 11.7 = ~2ms overlapped) -> the post-owned wall impact is UNVERIFIED
questions.opaque_append_nan_still_blocker: the owned-TILE side that entangled the prior runtime-KV failure is NOW FIXED (dtype + fp16 cache); the opaque-append-NaN finding predates that fix and must be RE-DIAGNOSED -- it may have been a downstream artifact of the tile bug
questions.would_clear_5pct_wd: PLAUSIBLY yes (~+9% if the gqa-era +8 tok/s transfers) -- but unverified on the owned route post-FO2

rationale: The KV-copy materialization is still ~1.5ms/token and impact was real in the gqa era. But two facts changed since the last runtime-KV attempt: (1) the owned-tile dtype bug (which entangled the prior 'append-NaN') is fixed, and (2) the owned route altered the overlap structure. Before committing to the hard runtime-KV implementation, run two CHEAP diagnostics: (a) a MAXC-shrink A/B on the OWNED route to confirm the KV-copy WALL impact post-FO2; (b) re-test the opaque append + owned tile (now fixed) + fp16 cache for byte-identical multi-step decode. If (a) shows >=5% wall and (b) no longer NaNs, reopen as RUNTIME_KV_REOPEN_JUSTIFIED.
